commit.message:
Add MXFP8 XPU feasibility analysis

Document the investigation into MXFP8 support for Intel XPU via sycl-tla:
- Survey of all Xe dispatch policies in sycl-tla (gap: no grouped+FP8+scaling)
- float_ue8m0_t support (software fallback only on Intel Xe; no CUDA PTX)
- Intel Xe hardware limits (no native blockscaled MXFP8 XMX instruction)
- Three implementation options with effort/quality tradeoffs
- Recommendation: Option A (software dequant pre-pass) now, Option B
  (new sycl-tla MainloopXeL1StagedGroupFP8Scaling) as upstream contribution

fix: preserve previous CSS/JS assets across deploys for Clarity recording fidelity

Clarity session recordings render as unstyled HTML because Vite generates
new content-hashed filenames each build, and GitHub Pages replaces all files
atomically — so CSS URLs referenced in recorded sessions 404 after the next
deploy.

Solution: download live site's CSS/JS assets before build, merge them into
dist/ after build. Old assets survive alongside new ones for up to 30 days,
giving Clarity recordings time to expire naturally.

- scripts/preserve-clarity-assets.mjs: --download fetches live assets,
  --merge copies cached assets into dist/assets/
- deploy.yml: GitHub Actions cache persists assets across runs
- Both steps are continue-on-error to never block deploys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: migrate to BAP/PP API scopes to resolve AADSTS65002 in corporate tenants (#11)

## Problem

The extension was requesting the
`https://service.flow.microsoft.com/user_impersonation` scope. In
corporate tenants where user consent is disabled by policy, this causes
an **AADSTS65002** preauthorization error, completely blocking sign-in
for enterprise developers.

## Solution

Switch to Microsoft first-party API scopes that are pre-authorized in
all Microsoft Entra tenants:

| Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `https://api.bap.microsoft.com/.default` | Environment listing (BAP
API) |
| `https://api.powerplatform.com/.default` | Cloud flows & flow runs
(Power Platform API) |

This is the same pattern used by Microsoft's own [Power Platform Tools
VS Code
extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=microsoft-IsvExpTools.powerplatform-vscode).
These scopes do not require admin consent or app registration.

## Changes

### `src/auth/AuthProvider.ts`
- Removes the single `service.flow.microsoft.com` scope
- Adds `getBapToken()` and `getPpApiToken()` — separate tokens for the
two APIs
- Maps sovereign cloud API base URLs to their corresponding BAP/PP API
scopes
- Exposes `bapBaseUrl` and `ppApiBaseUrl` getters for the client to use
- `signIn()`: fixed `forceNewSession` → `createIfNone` (avoids forcing
re-auth when already signed in)

### `src/api/PowerAutomateClient.ts`
- `listEnvironments`: migrated to BAP API (`api.bap.microsoft.com`)
- `listFlows`, `getFlow`, `listFlowRuns`: migrated to Power Platform API
(`api.powerplatform.com`)
- Methods not yet supported by the PP API now throw a clear "not yet
available" error (handled by the `registerTools.ts` catch wrapper —
Copilot Chat will see a user-friendly message)

### `package.json`
- Default `apiBaseUrl` updated to `https://api.powerplatform.com`
- Sovereign cloud enum values updated to PP API URLs

### `docs/azure-app-registration.md` & `README.md`
- Docs rewritten to reflect no app registration requirement
- Removes stale `service.flow.microsoft.com` references

## Temporarily reduced API surface

Some API methods now throw "not yet available" until the Power Platform
API achieves parity with the legacy `service.flow.microsoft.com`
endpoints. These are clearly documented in the client. Affected
operations:
- `listConnections`, `createOrUpdateFlow`, `setFlowState`,
`addFlowToSolution`
- `getFlowRunActions`, `getFlowRunActionInputsOutputs`
- `resubmitFlowRun`, `cancelFlowRun`, `getFlowHttpSchema`,
`getFlowTriggerUrl`, `triggerFlow`

**Working:** `listEnvironments`, `listFlows`, `getFlow`, `listFlowRuns`

## Supersedes

Closes and supersedes #10 — this PR solves the same corporate-tenant
auth problem at the root cause (wrong scope) rather than adding an Azure
CLI workaround.

## Testing

- [ ] Sign in with a personal Microsoft account
- [ ] Sign in with a corporate account in a tenant with user consent
disabled
- [ ] Environments list loads correctly
- [ ] Flows list loads for an environment
- [ ] Flow runs load for a selected flow
- [ ] Unavailable tools return a clear error message in Copilot Chat

---------

Co-authored-by: Logan Ash <Ash.Logan@mayo.edu>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(task-116): force-push repair branch on reruns; fix clipped_text edge case (#147)

Two edge cases surfaced during the live TASK-116 repair-lane smoke tests
that the earlier follow-up commits didn't fully close.

## Changes

- **Force-push repair branch on reruns** — `cmd_finalize` now uses `git
push --force -u origin` instead of a plain push. When a run successfully
pushes the repair branch but then fails to create the PR (as happened in
run `24164768702`), a subsequent forced rerun rebuilds the local branch
from `FETCH_HEAD` and the plain push is rejected because the remote
already has diverged history. Repair branches are automation-owned so
force push is safe.

  ```python
  # before
  git("push", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  # after
  git("push", "--force", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  ```

- **`clipped_text` edge case** — when `limit <= len(ellipsis)`, now
returns `normalized[:limit]` (plain truncation) instead of
`ellipsis[:limit]`, which would emit a meaningless `".."` or `"."`.
Unreachable with the current `MAX_REPLACEMENT_SUMMARY_CHARS = 2500`
caller, but the guard is now semantically correct.

  ```python
  # before
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return ellipsis[:limit]   # e.g. ".." or "." — confusing
  # after
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return normalized[:limit]  # plain truncation
  ```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ConfigurationBinder failing to bind empty array to constructor parameter (#126470)

Fix #126312

When binding an empty JSON array (`[]`) to a constructor parameter of
type `string[]`, `ConfigurationBinder` threw:

```
ArgumentException: Object of type 'System.String' cannot be converted to type 'System.String[]'.
```

## Root Cause

In `BindParameter`, the `BindingPoint` was initialized with
`config.GetSection(parameterName).Value` — the config section's string
value. For an empty JSON array `[]`, this value is `""` (empty string).
This caused `BindInstance` to skip the empty-collection creation code
path, which requires `bindingPoint.Value` to be `null`. The empty string
value was then returned as the parameter value, causing the
`ArgumentException` when the constructor was invoked via reflection.

For comparison, property binding correctly initializes the
`BindingPoint` with `property.GetValue(instance)` (typically `null` for
uninitialized arrays), so the empty-collection path works.

## Fix

Remove the `initialValue` argument from the `BindingPoint` constructor
in `BindParameter`, so it defaults to `null`. This is correct because:

- Constructor parameters have no "existing value" to preserve
- The config section's value is already read inside `BindInstance` from
the `config` parameter directly — passing it as `initialValue` was
redundant
- For simple types (string, int, etc.), `TryConvertValue` succeeds and
overwrites the initial value regardless

## Test

Added `TestBindingEmptyArrayToConstructorParameter` — reproduces the
exact scenario from the issue (empty JSON array bound to a `string[]`
constructor parameter with a null default).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Local on the NodePort, this always reaches the pihole pod on control10.

all 3 pods.

previous commit), drop the now-unneeded TraefikService.

commit.message:
ExitPolicy: policy-driven child VMEXIT dispatch

- Add child_exit_trap lookup before child dispatch match block
  (reads ExitPolicy from child domain's capability)
- Gate forward_child_exit calls with policy check:
  EXCEPTION_NMI (exceptions), CPUID (non-Themis leaves),
  EPT_VIOLATION (non-doorbell), and catch-all all check trap flag
- Extract shared local handlers from dom0 path:
  reinject_exception, handle_cpuid_local, handle_rdmsr_local,
  handle_wrmsr_local — used by both dom0 and child trap=false paths
- Dom0 dispatch now delegates to shared handlers (no duplication)
- fix forward_child_exit to read read_set from ExitPolicy
  (was incorrectly using InterruptPolicy for exit forwarding)
- Always-local exits unchanged: EXTERNAL_INTERRUPT (interrupt routing),
  INTERRUPT_WINDOW (PIR drain), VMX_PREEMPTION_TIMER (internal),
  INIT_SIGNAL (cross-core), VMCALL (capability engine), XSETBV
- Themis CPUID leaves + 0x15 TSC always handled locally regardless
  of policy (capavisor identity)

commit.message:
Cache parsed remote JSON-LD contexts to avoid re-parsing on each edit

The converter was re-parsing fetched remote @context documents (e.g.
schema.org, ~300KB of JSON-LD) on every call to convert_with_loader.
The WasmFetchLoader cached the raw string but not the parsed result,
so every keystroke in the demo triggered a full parse of the 300KB
context document — the actual root cause of >1s latency.

Fix: thread-local PARSED_CTX_CACHE keyed by URL stores the parsed
JsonLdVal.  First access parses; subsequent accesses return the
cached value.  Zero signature changes to existing functions.

Also: make JsonLdVal public and expose clear_parsed_context_cache()
for cache invalidation.

fix: FT2 toolbar responsive — sections wrap instead of overflowing

commit.message:
fix(hosted): custom response converter for multi-tool-call support

The default ResponseAPIMessagesNonStreamResponseConverter only emits the
first tool_call per AIMessage, silently dropping the rest. QPrisma's agent
routinely makes 2-3 parallel tool calls per turn, creating orphaned
ToolMessage outputs that Foundry rejects as 'invalid format'.

QPrismaNonStreamResponseConverter fixes this by:
- Emitting ALL FunctionToolCallItemResource per AIMessage
- Tracking emitted call_ids and dropping orphaned tool outputs
- Skipping restore_media_context node (returns conversation history, not output)
- Filtering HumanMessage/SystemMessage as defense-in-depth
- Handling AIMessage with both text content and tool_calls

Wired into QPrismaStateConverter via create_non_stream_response_converter
factory callback pattern.

17 new tests covering multi-tool, orphan guard, node filtering, HITL,
edge cases, and realistic QPrisma query flows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: P_Bayes always 1.0000 in integration layer due to per-pair normalization (#97)

* Initial plan

* fix: globally normalize P_Bayes in integration layer to prevent 1.0000 display

Root cause: _compute_bayesian_posteriors normalized within each (A,B) pair.
Since composition_table has exactly one C per pair, the per-pair normalization
always yielded probability=1.0, causing the integration formula to always show
P_Bayes=1.0000 — inconsistent with the probability tree (which normalized
across all transitions).

Fix:
- _compute_bayesian_posteriors now returns (weights_dict, Z_pair) — raw
  unnormalized weights rather than pre-divided probabilities.
- run_bayesian_inference accepts weights+Z_pair and stores partition_function
  as the true unnormalized Z, not sum(normalized probs).
- run_dual_inference uses a two-pass approach: collect all raw results, compute
  Z_global = sum of all posterior_weights, then normalize each probability as
  posterior_weight/Z_global before calling integrate(). This makes P_Bayes
  consistent with the probability tree display.
- confidence_final is now correctly bounded by P_Bayes (≤ P_Bayes always).

Tests:
- Updated existing tests to use new _compute_bayesian_posteriors/
  run_bayesian_inference signatures.
- Added regression tests: P_Bayes≠1.0 with multiple transitions,
  P_Bayes consistent with probability tree, confidence_final≤P_Bayes,
  partition_function equals Z_global.

commit.message:
feat(#28): add ingredient normalization backend (#127)

Implements POST /api/recipes/normalize-ingredients endpoint that calls
Gemini to detect ambiguous ingredient lines and suggest normalized
alternatives with confidence scoring.

BDD Scenarios:
- Scenario 1: Ambiguous ingredients → normalization suggestions returned
- Scenario 2: Low-confidence suggestions (< 0.6) → filtered out
- Scenario 3: Empty ingredient list → returns empty list gracefully
- Scenario 4: API failure → returns empty list (graceful degradation)

Changes:
- IngredientNormalizationRequest (new): request DTO with ingredients + recipeName
- IngredientNormalization (new): single suggestion with index/original/normalized/reason/confidence
- IngredientNormalizationResponse (new): response DTO wrapping list of normalizations
- IngredientNormalizationService (new): Gemini call, JSON schema, confidence filter
- RecipeController: 4th service arg + POST /normalize-ingredients endpoint
- RecipeControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- RefineInstructionsControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- SuggestFieldsControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- NormalizeIngredientsControllerTest (new): controller-level BDD tests
- IngredientNormalizationServiceTest (new): service-level unit tests

Closes #28 (backend slice)

commit.message:
Fix cross-realm prototype resolution for Map/Set constructors

When Reflect.construct(Map, [], ChildRealmCtor) is used, the prototype
should come from the child realm's Map.prototype, not the parent's.

Two fixes:
1. Function constructor: check new_target's __realm_id__ to set correct
   dynamic_origin_global for child realm functions
2. default_intrinsic_prototype_for_constructor: when origin_global is a
   child realm and constructor is a bare VmNativeFunction, read prototype
   from child VM's native function properties instead of parent's

Fixes: Map/proto-from-ctor-realm.js, Set/proto-from-ctor-realm.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
